## UAC-style polish + fixes for the install race

Small follow-up to v0.5.0 with two new user-facing features and
one important fix for the install-in-place flow.

## Audio cue when the dialog appears

New `[audio]` config section, default sound `dialog-warning`:

```toml
[audio]
sound_name = "dialog-warning"
```

Played asynchronously through `canberra-gtk-play` (part of
`libcanberra`, installed by default on most Linux desktops). Fires
right before the cosmic event loop starts so users get auditory
feedback that something needs attention even if the dialog hasn't
painted yet. Set to `""` to disable.

Common values from the freedesktop sound naming spec:
`dialog-warning`, `bell`, `message`, `dialog-question`. Resolution
goes through the user's icon/sound theme so distros and users can
customize without rebuilding Sentinel.

## Logind session metadata in audit logs

Auth-outcome events now include the requesting process's logind
session context:

```text
event=auth.allow source=agent user=alice action=org.fd.policykit.exec
process=topgrade latency_ms=2376
session_type=wayland session_class=user session_remote=0
```

Three new fields:

- **`session_type`** — `wayland` / `x11` / `tty` (whatever logind says)
- **`session_class`** — `user` / `greeter` / `manager`
- **`session_remote`** — `1` if SSH or other remote, `0` if local console

Trivially queryable: `journalctl ... | grep session_remote=1`
finds every remote escalation across the whole system.

Implementation reads `/run/systemd/sessions/<id>` directly (plain
`KEY=value` text files) — no D-Bus, no async, no libsystemd C
dep. The PAM module recovers the requesting process's
`XDG_SESSION_ID` from `/proc/<pid>/environ` (the privileged
binary it's loaded into scrubbed its own copy).

## Install-time competing-agent race fix

If a session-supervised polkit agent (`cosmic-osd`,
`polkit-{gnome,kde}-authentication-agent-1`, etc.) was already
registered when you ran `pkexec ./install.sh`, the in-place agent
restart from v0.5.0 would silently lose the race and Sentinel
would fall back to the slower PAM-fork dialog path instead of
the fast bypass.

v0.5.1 fixes this two ways:

1. **`install.sh`** now pre-computes env vars before the
   kill-and-spawn race window, kills known competing agents in
   the same pass, and verifies registration succeeded by tailing
   the journal — not just by checking the bypass socket bound
   (the socket binds before registration is even attempted).

2. **The agent itself** retries `RegisterAuthenticationAgent` up
   to 8 times with 500 ms backoff when the error is specifically
   "An authentication agent already exists for the given subject".
   Other errors (bad session id, polkitd down, etc.) propagate
   immediately because they don't self-heal by waiting.

For session-supervised competitors that hard-respawn forever
(cosmic-osd is the canonical case), the only reliable workaround
is to disable the competitor outright:

```bash
pkexec chmod -x /usr/bin/cosmic-osd
```

This loses cosmic-osd's volume/brightness OSDs but keeps Sentinel
as the sole polkit agent. The install script now logs this hint
when it detects the situation, so users aren't left guessing.

## Other changes

- **Compositor compatibility table** in the README expanded with
  Niri, Wayfire, River explicitly. Honest about which are tested
  by the maintainer (only cosmic-comp) versus expected to work
  based on protocol support.
- **Hyprland config snippet** at `packaging/hyprland/sentinel.conf`
  — optional `layerrule` entries that give the dialog surface a
  fade-in animation and blur, matching the polish other UAC-style
  prompts have.
- **Keyboard accessibility** documented in `app.rs::subscription` —
  Tab cycles between Allow/Deny (iced default), Enter/Space
  activates the focused button, Escape always denies. We
  deliberately do NOT auto-focus the Allow button on surface
  creation — a sticky-keys user mashing Return must not be able
  to auto-allow. Combined with `randomize_buttons`, this is the
  anti-clickthrough story.
- **Repo polish**: dependabot config (weekly cargo + Actions
  bumps), `SECURITY.md` for vuln reporting, `cargo audit` in CI,
  aarch64 build job (free arm64 runners), issue + PR templates,
  release.yml concurrency guard.

## Verify

```bash
pgrep -af sentinel-polkit-agent
pkexec true                                # listen for the audio cue
journalctl -t sentinel-polkit-agent -t pam_sentinel \
    --output=cat --since "1 minute ago" \
    | grep "event=auth"                    # see structured logs +
                                           # session_type/class/remote
```

## Wiki

Full installation, configuration, architecture, and troubleshooting
docs at <https://github.com/atayozcan/sentinel/wiki>.
